data.title: Total Dose Infusion of Iron Dextran Versus Oral Iron for Treating Iron Deficiency Anemia in Pregnant Women: a Randomized Controlled Trial
data.brief_summary: Iron deficiency is the most prevalent nutritional deficiency and the most common cause of anemia .It is characterized by a defect in hemoglobin synthesis, resulting in red blood cells that are abnormally small (microcytic) and contain a decreased amount of hemoglobin (hypochromic).
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Hemoglobin \< 10 g/dL
* Gestational age between 14-28 weeks.
* Willingness to participate and signing the informed consent form.

Exclusion Criteria:

* Iron overload or disturbances in utilization of iron
* Decompensated liver cirrhosis and active hepatitis
* Active acute or
endpoints: The change in Hemoglobin concentration from baseline to 8 weeks therapy
